reasoning: While programmatic signals show strong content recall (0.97), the document exhibits a critical failure in image rendering (95% broken) and major structural corruption. The use of layout tables has fractured sentences and destroyed the heading hierarchy, leading to a severely compromised reading order that fails accessibility standards.

concerns.description: Image placeholder ratio 95%: 37/39 images are broken/missing (transparent 1x1 placeholders)

concerns.analysis: Several major section headings and subheadings from the source document (Pages 17 and 18) have been incorrectly converted into plain text within table cells rather than being identified as heading elements. For example, 'Modernize and centralize IT operations' (a major H2 heading on Page 18) and subheadings like 'New Applications', 'Constituent Portal', and 'First Responder Emerging Technologies Program' are all embedded as text inside tables 25 and 26. This effectively removes them from the doc
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Modernize and centralize IT operations (Page 18, major heading); New Applications (Page 17, subheading).
concerns.output_quote: The text 'Modernize and centralize IT operations' is used as a table caption for element index 26, and subheadings like 'New Applications' are plain text in table cells (Element index 25, Row 1, Cell 1).
concerns.location: Sections 'Cultivate our IT workforce' and 'Modernize and centralize IT operations', element indices 25 and 26.

concerns.analysis: Tables 23, 25, and 26 are structurally corrupted. Instead of representing structured data, these tables were used to manage multi-column layouts from the source, resulting in single sentences and list items being split across different rows and cells. For example, in Table 23, a sentence is broken between Row 1 Cell 1 and Row 1 Cell 2. This is a fundamental failure of table structure identification.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Built an inspection reporting tool for inspectors to record all aspects of an asphalt surface treatment on iPads, even when working in remote environments without a cellular signal. (Page 16)
concerns.output_quote:
Row 1, Cell 1: '...even when working in remote'
Row 1, Cell 2: 'environments without a cellular signal...'
concerns.location: Section 'Leveraging IT Resources for Better Decision Making', element index 23.

concerns.analysis: The reading order is severely disrupted by the corrupted table structures in elements 23, 25, and 26. Because sentences are split across table cells, a standard top-to-bottom or left-to-right reading of the table results in fragmented and logically broken information flow. This makes the content incomprehensible for users, particularly those using screen readers who rely on logical linear flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Built a mobile-first, public-facing request system for the N.C. Division of Motor Vehicles’ License and Theft Bureau, transforming its operations with more than 10,000 submissions in the first nine months of operations (Page 17)
concerns.output_quote:
Row 1, Cell 1: '...more than 10,000 submissions in'
Row 1, Cell 2: 'the first nine months of operations'
concerns.location: Section 'Cultivate our IT workforce', element index 25.

action_items: Restore the 37 missing images and graphics.
Deconstruct layout tables and replace with semantic HTML blocks to restore reading order.
Manually tag broken headings (e.g., 'Modernize and centralize IT operations') as H2/H3 elements.
Rejoin fragmented sentences currently split across table cells.
